description_long: This is a large, military-style helicopter with a robust, elongated fuselage and a prominent cockpit at the front. The body is primarily painted in a muted, camouflaged gray-green color scheme, with subtle weathering and panel lines visible along the surface. The helicopter features a main rotor with several long, slender blades and a tail boom ending in an angled tail rotor. The landing gear consists of sturdy, sponson-mounted wheels rather than traditional skids, suggesting heavy-duty or transport capabilities. The cockpit windows are multi-paneled, slightly tinted, and wrap around the front, providing wide visibility for the pilots. Various hatches, antennas, and external equipment are visible, indicating a complex and functional design suitable for urban or tactical operations.
description: A large, camouflaged military helicopter with a multi-paneled cockpit, long main rotor blades, and robust sponson-mounted landing gear. The body is detailed with weathering, panel lines, and external equipment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, multi-paneled cockpit windows, the main rotor hub, and the symmetrical sponson-mounted landing gear.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, camouflaged paint, and the full length of the main and tail rotors.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetrical arrangement of the landing gear and sponsons.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cockpit, fuselage, and the angled tail rotor at the end of the boom.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, slender main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, colored dark gray with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, angled vertically, with four short blades, colored gray with no visible markings or protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: This helicopter uses sponson-mounted landing gear with wheels, not traditional skids. The gear is robust, metallic, and painted in the same camouflaged color as the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Five cockpit windows: three large, forward-facing panes and two angled side panes, all slightly tinted blue with dark framing and no visible markings.
